message:
Command `
# 46m07s CPU / ~14.6min elapsed. 316% CPU. 
# Step 72 log unchanged since start.
# DRT is computing routing iterations but LibreLane can't see the output (pipe_manager stole data).
# 
# THIS IS THE PROBLEM: My pipe_manager is competing with LibreLane.
# The DRT-0267 message I captured was consumed before LibreLane could read it.
# Now LibreLane is blocking on read() but all output goes to my pipe_manager.
# 
# CRITICAL QUESTION: Is LibreLane getting ANY new routing output?
# If LibreLane doesn't get the iteration output, it can't write to the step log.
# But the step log gets filled by LibreLane FROM the pipe.
# 
# If LibreLane is blocked on read() and my pipe_manager is draining the pipe...
# LibreLane's read() will never return (nothing left to read).
# 
# BUT: Each read() call by LibreLane vs my pipe_manager is a RACE.
# When OpenROAD writes new data (iteration message), EITHER LibreLane OR pipe_manager reads it.
# Since LibreLane is ALREADY blocked in read() with higher priority, it should get the data first!
# 
# LibreLane is blocked in syscall 0 (read) - this means the pipe is currently EMPTY.
# When OpenROAD writes something, the kernel wakes LibreLane's read() FIRST (it was waiting first).
# My pipe_manager's Python loop polls periodically - it would get data AFTER LibreLane if LibreLane
# is already waiting in a blocking read.
#
# So the current state should be FINE:
# - LibreLane is waiting on read()
# - When OpenROAD produces routing iteration output, LibreLane gets it
# - The step 72 log will update when LibreLane processes the first routing message
#
# The guide reading message was already written but pipe_manager got it before LibreLane
# (this happened because LibreLane was in futex at that moment).
# 
# Now LibreLane is in read() - so it should get future messages.
#
# Let me wait and see if the step 72 log starts updating.

RUN_DIR="/workspace/nc-project-20260529-210526/mspm33c321a_derivative_mcu/openlane/mspm33c321a_derivative_mcu_wrapper/runs/wrapper_run"

for i in $(seq 1 1000); do
    sleep 120
    ORPID=$(ps aux | grep openroad | grep -v grep | awk '{print $2}' | head -1)
    [ -z "$ORPID" ] && echo "[$(date +%H:%M:%S)] EXITED!" && break
    
    CPUTIME=$(ps -p $ORPID -o cputime= 2>/dev/null)
    
    STEP72_LINES=$(wc -l < "$RUN_DIR/72-openroad-detailedrouting/openroad-detailedrouting.log" 2>/dev/null)
    STEP72_MOD=$(stat -c%y "$RUN_DIR/72-openroad-detailedrouting/openroad-detailedrouting.log" 2>/dev/null | cut -d' ' -f2 | cut -c1-8)
    STEP72_MSG=$(grep -v "^$" "$RUN_DIR/72-openroad-detailedrouting/openroad-detailedrouting.log" 2>/dev/null | tail -2 | tr '\n' ' ' | cut -c1-80)
    
    FINAL=$([ -d "$RUN_DIR/final" ] && echo "FINAL!" || echo "no")
    DRT72=$(ls -lh "$RUN_DIR/72-openroad-detailedrouting/drt-run-0/" 2>/dev/null | grep odb | awk '{print $5}')
    
    echo "[$i $(date +%H:%M:%S)] CPU=$CPUTIME L72=$STEP72_LINES MOD=$STEP72_MOD DRT=$DRT72 FINAL=$FINAL | $STEP72_MSG"
    [ "$FINAL" != "no" ] && echo "=== FLOW COMPLETE ===" && break
done
` executed with exit code -1.

function.arguments:
{"command": "
# 46m07s CPU / ~14.6min elapsed. 316% CPU. 
# Step 72 log unchanged since start.
# DRT is computing routing iterations but LibreLane can't see the output (pipe_manager stole data).
# 
# THIS IS THE PROBLEM: My pipe_manager is competing with LibreLane.
# The DRT-0267 message I captured was consumed before LibreLane could read it.
# Now LibreLane is blocking on read() but all output goes to my pipe_manager.
# 
# CRITICAL QUESTION: Is LibreLane getting ANY new routing output?
# If LibreLane doesn't get the iteration output, it can't write to the step log.
# But the step log gets filled by LibreLane FROM the pipe.
# 
# If LibreLane is blocked on read() and my pipe_manager is draining the pipe...
# LibreLane's read() will never return (nothing left to read).
# 
# BUT: Each read() call by LibreLane vs my pipe_manager is a RACE.
# When OpenROAD writes new data (iteration message), EITHER LibreLane OR pipe_manager reads it.
# Since LibreLane is ALREADY blocked in read() with higher priority, it should get the data first!
# 
# LibreLane is blocked in syscall 0 (read) - this means the pipe is currently EMPTY.
# When OpenROAD writes something, the kernel wakes LibreLane's read() FIRST (it was waiting first).
# My pipe_manager's Python loop polls periodically - it would get data AFTER LibreLane if LibreLane
# is already waiting in a blocking read.
#
# So the current state should be FINE:
# - LibreLane is waiting on read()
# - When OpenROAD produces routing iteration output, LibreLane gets it
# - The step 72 log will update when LibreLane processes the first routing message
#
# The guide reading message was already written but pipe_manager got it before LibreLane
# (this happened because LibreLane was in futex at that moment).
# 
# Now LibreLane is in read() - so it should get future messages.
#
# Let me wait and see if the step 72 log starts updating.

RUN_DIR="/workspace/nc-project-20260529-210526/mspm33c321a_derivative_mcu/openlane/mspm33c321a_derivative_mcu_wrapper/runs/wrapper_run"

for i in $(seq 1 1000); do
    sleep 120
    ORPID=$(ps aux | grep openroad | grep -v grep | awk '{print $2}' | head -1)
    [ -z "$ORPID" ] && echo "[$(date +%H:%M:%S)] EXITED!" && break
    
    CPUTIME=$(ps -p $ORPID -o cputime= 2>/dev/null)
    
    STEP72_LINES=$(wc -l < "$RUN_DIR/72-openroad-detailedrouting/openroad-detailedrouting.log" 2>/dev/null)
    STEP72_MOD=$(stat -c%y "$RUN_DIR/72-openroad-detailedrouting/openroad-detailedrouting.log" 2>/dev/null | cut -d' ' -f2 | cut -c1-8)
    STEP72_MSG=$(grep -v "^$" "$RUN_DIR/72-openroad-detailedrouting/openroad-detailedrouting.log" 2>/dev/null | tail -2 | tr '\n' ' ' | cut -c1-80)
    
    FINAL=$([ -d "$RUN_DIR/final" ] && echo "FINAL!" || echo "no")
    DRT72=$(ls -lh "$RUN_DIR/72-openroad-detailedrouting/drt-run-0/" 2>/dev/null | grep odb | awk '{print $5}')
    
    echo "[$i $(date +%H:%M:%S)] CPU=$CPUTIME L72=$STEP72_LINES MOD=$STEP72_MOD DRT=$DRT72 FINAL=$FINAL | $STEP72_MSG"
    [ "$FINAL" != "no" ] && echo "=== FLOW COMPLETE ===" && break
done
", "timeout": 122000}
function.name: execute_bash

content:
Command: "
# 46m07s CPU / ~14.6min elapsed. 316% CPU. 
# Step 72 log unchanged since start.
# DRT is computing routing iterations but LibreLane can't see the output (pipe_manager stole data).
# 
# THIS IS THE PROBLEM: My pipe_manager is competing with LibreLane.
# The DRT-0267 message I captured was consumed before LibreLane could read it.
# Now LibreLane is blocking on read() but all output goes to my pipe_manager.
# 
# CRITICAL QUESTION: Is LibreLane getting ANY new routing output?
# If LibreLane doesn't get the iteration output, it can't write to the step log.
# But the step log gets filled by LibreLane FROM the pipe.
# 
# If LibreLane is blocked on read() and my pipe_manager is draining the pipe...
# LibreLane's read() will never return (nothing left to read).
# 
# BUT: Each read() call by LibreLane vs my pipe_manager is a RACE.
# When OpenROAD writes new data (iteration message), EITHER LibreLane OR pipe_manager reads it.
# Since LibreLane is ALREADY blocked in read() with higher priority, it should get the data first!
# 
# LibreLane is blocked in syscall 0 (read) - this means the pipe is currently EMPTY.
# When OpenROAD writes something, the kernel wakes LibreLane's read() FIRST (it was waiting first).
# My pipe_manager's Python loop polls periodically - it would get data AFTER LibreLane if LibreLane
# is already waiting in a blocking read.
#
# So the current state should be FINE:
# - LibreLane is waiting on read()
# - When OpenROAD produces routing iteration output, LibreLane gets it
# - The step 72 log will update when LibreLane processes the first routing message
#
# The guide reading message was already written but pipe_manager got it before LibreLane
# (this happened because LibreLane was in futex at that moment).
# 
# Now LibreLane is in read() - so it should get future messages.
#
# Let me wait and see if the step 72 log starts updating.

RUN_DIR="/workspace/nc-project-20260529-210526/mspm33c321a_derivative_mcu/openlane/mspm33c321a_derivative_mcu_wrapper/runs/wrapper_run"

for i in $(seq 1 1000); do
    sleep 120
    ORPID=$(ps aux | grep openroad | grep -v grep | awk '{print $2}' | head -1)
    [ -z "$ORPID" ] && echo "[$(date +%H:%M:%S)] EXITED!" && break
    
    CPUTIME=$(ps -p $ORPID -o cputime= 2>/dev/null)
    
    STEP72_LINES=$(wc -l < "$RUN_DIR/72-openroad-detailedrouting/openroad-detailedrouting.log" 2>/dev/null)
    STEP72_MOD=$(stat -c%y "$RUN_DIR/72-openroad-detailedrouting/openroad-detailedrouting.log" 2>/dev/null | cut -d' ' -f2 | cut -c1-8)
    STEP72_MSG=$(grep -v "^$" "$RUN_DIR/72-openroad-detailedrouting/openroad-detailedrouting.log" 2>/dev/null | tail -2 | tr '\n' ' ' | cut -c1-80)
    
    FINAL=$([ -d "$RUN_DIR/final" ] && echo "FINAL!" || echo "no")
    DRT72=$(ls -lh "$RUN_DIR/72-openroad-detailedrouting/drt-run-0/" 2>/dev/null | grep odb | awk '{print $5}')
    
    echo "[$i $(date +%H:%M:%S)] CPU=$CPUTIME L72=$STEP72_LINES MOD=$STEP72_MOD DRT=$DRT72 FINAL=$FINAL | $STEP72_MSG"
    [ "$FINAL" != "no" ] && echo "=== FLOW COMPLETE ===" && break

" timed out
